what_it_does: Adds idempotent interpretive text columns to ledger.entry (contribution, summary_for_embedding, intention_for_embedding, interp_model, interp_at) and ledger.symbol (summary_for_embedding, interp_model, interp_at), and creates a partial index entry_interp_gate on ledger.entry(run_id, coverage_state) where what_it_does is null to act as an interpretive completeness gate.
observations: Uses ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS for idempotent DDL.
Adds a contribution text column plus two embedding-text faces (summary_for_embedding, intention_for_embedding) to ledger.entry.
Adds only summary_for_embedding plus interp_model and interp_at to ledger.symbol.
Creates a partial index keyed by run_id and coverage_state filtering rows where what_it_does IS NULL.
Comments state that vectors will be added later by an embedder service on port :8001.
Assumes structural columns from migration 0011 already exist.
standouts: Partial index used as a deterministic interpretive completeness gate.
Separation of embedding text faces from actual vector storage.
Idempotent migration pattern with IF NOT EXISTS.
conventions: Idempotent DDL using IF NOT EXISTS.
Migration file prefixed with a sequential number and descriptive name.
Inline comments explaining purpose and dependencies.
concerns: The index references columns (run_id, coverage_state, what_it_does) that are not defined in this file and are assumed from migration 0011.
No explicit transaction wrapper or down-migration is present.
Partial index condition depends on what_it_does being nullable, which is not visible here.
notes: This is the interpretive schema patch that prepares ledger tables for model-generated text and an unprocessed-entry gate index.
questions: What are the exact definitions of run_id, coverage_state, and what_it_does in migration 0011?
How is interp_at populated—by trigger, application code, or manual update?
What is the embedder service schema and when/how are the vector columns added?
Is contribution intended to be nullable, and is there a uniqueness constraint per run/file?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the file: it adds interpretive text columns and a completeness gate index as described.
apparent_themes: Separation of semantic text from vector embeddings
intention_signals: It aims to enable a model pass to write neutral interpretive summaries and contribution statements into the ledger, while deferring vector generation to a separate embedder service.
novelty: The 'interpretive layer' concept with dedicated embedding-text faces and a partial index acting as a processing gate.
contribution: Provides the database columns and index needed to store and query model-generated interpretive text for ledger entries and symbols, and to identify entries still awaiting interpretation.
summary_for_embedding: Migration 0012 adds idempotent interpretive text columns (contribution, summary_for_embedding, intention_for_embedding, interp_model, interp_at) to ledger.entry and ledger.symbol and creates a partial index on ledger.entry(run_id, coverage_state) where what_it_does is null to serve as an interpretive completeness gate.
intention_for_embedding: It prepares the ledger schema to receive neutral model-written interpretive metadata and to efficiently find entries that have not yet been interpreted.
